sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root בָּתַר).-

(1) election, choice; hence whatever is most choice, most excellent, best. Always in const. st., Isa 22:7, מִבְתַר עֲמָקַיִךְ “thy most choice valleys;” Isa 37:24, מִבְתַר בְּרֹושָׁיו “its most beautiful fir-trees;” Jer 22:7 Gen 23:6 Exo 15:4 Eze 23:7 24:4 . In an inverted order, עַם מִבְתָרָיו “his most choice people,” Dan 11:15.

(2) [Mibhar], pr.n. m. 1Ch 11:38.
